parts: Plexipus cuneifolius is commonly known as the Texas buckeye, and its medicinal parts include the bark, roots, and seeds. However, it's worth noting that the toxic properties of the plant may outweigh any potential medicinal benefits. The bark and roots of the Texas buckeye contain saponins, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The seeds, on the other hand, contain the toxic compound cyanogenic glycoside, which can be toxic if ingested. Due to its toxicity, the Texas buckeye should be handled with caution and its use should be guided by a qualified medical professional.

The health benefits of Solenostemon cuneifolius include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
